message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Feature: validate_nesting param on Context. core.py: Context.__init__ (add validate_nesting param + self.validate_nesting attr + self._children list), Context.close() (validate children empty, raise RuntimeError if not, and remove self from parent._children), Context.__exit__ (calls close via existing depth logic), Context._make_sub_context (inherit validate_nesting to child, register child in parent._children). shell_completion.py: _resolve_context() and the make_context calls within it must pass validate_nesting=False explicitly.

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Feature: fix resource leak in shell completion. In shell_completion.py: rewrite _resolve_context() to wrap cli.make_context()/cmd.make_context() calls in 'with' statements (3 call sites: root ctx, non-chained sub_ctx, chained sub_sub_ctx) so Context.__exit__ closes file handles. In core.py: only a docstring tweak on Context.exit() versionchanged note (no behavioral change).

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: I own shell_completion.py. I'm rewriting _resolve_context to wrap make_context calls in 'with' statements; I'll also add validate_nesting=False to every make_context call site there per your SPEC below.

type: DEFER
files: src/click/core.py
spec: In Context.exit() docstring only: change the versionchanged:: 8.2 note from 'Force closing of callbacks registered with :meth:`call_on_close` before exiting the CLI.' to 'Callbacks and context managers registered with :meth:`call_on_close` and :meth:`with_resource` are closed before exiting.' Purely a docstring wording fix, no behavioral/code change needed for my feature - fold it in wherever convenient alongside your Context changes.
note: trivial, non-behavioral doc tweak for my resource-leak fix

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/core.py
note: I'll own core.py: implement validate_nesting param/tracking/validation, and also fold in your docstring versionchanged note on Context.close()/__exit__ per your SURVEY.

type: DEFER
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
spec: In _resolve_context(cli, ctx_args, prog_name, args): the initial call 'ctx = cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args)' and both make_context calls inside the while loop (the non-chained branch 'ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True)' and the chained branch 'sub_ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, allow_extra_args=True, allow_interspersed_args=False, resilient_parsing=True)') must all explicitly pass validate_nesting=False as a kwarg, so Context objects created during shell completion never trigger the new nested-context RuntimeError validation (which fires in Context.close()/__exit__ when a parent is closed while children remain open — relevant if you wrap these in 'with' blocks for cleanup, since completion resolution intentionally keeps parent contexts referenced/open while building child contexts). Just add validate_nesting=False to each of those 3 make_context call sites (or make sure it flows through if you restructure the function). No other functions in this file create Context objects directly that I'm aware of.
note: make_context forwards **extra kwargs straight to Context(); validate_nesting=False is a plain kwarg like resilient_parsing

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Acknowledged your SPEC: in _resolve_context, I will pass validate_nesting=False to cli.make_context(...) and to both cmd.make_context(...) call sites (non-chained sub_ctx and chained sub_sub_ctx branches), on top of the 'with' wrapping. Proceeding to implement now.

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/core.py
note: Acknowledged your SPEC: I'll update the versionchanged:: 8.2 note on Context.close()/__exit__ docstring to your wording. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Implemented: _resolve_context wraps all 3 make_context call sites in 'with' blocks and passes validate_nesting=False to each. Waiting on your core.py DONE to run the full test suite.

type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py
note: core.py done: validate_nesting param, self._children tracking, close() validation raising RuntimeError, _make_sub_context inheritance, docstring updates. Heads up: git diff on my end shows shell_completion.py as unmodified — looks like we're in separate filesystem copies, not a shared one, so coordination messages don't actually merge into one tree. I'll add validate_nesting=False to the 3 make_context call sites in my own copy of shell_completion.py so my patch.txt is self-contained/complete for my feature; this shouldn't affect your patch since we're isolated.

type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Full feature complete, all 614 tests pass, patch.txt written. Since filesystems appear isolated, I included the validate_nesting=False additions to shell_completion.py's 3 make_context calls in my own patch as well, on top of core.py — shouldn't conflict with your separate copy.

type: DONE
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Good catch — confirmed separate filesystem copies here too (my core.py has no validate_nesting). Reverting validate_nesting=False from my shell_completion.py patch since my patch.txt must stand alone against the unmodified base repo and would TypeError otherwise. My patch now only does the with-statement resource-leak fix. No action needed on your end.

type: DONE
files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
note: Finished and verified: shell_completion.py resource-leak fix (with-statements, no validate_nesting since we're isolated), core.py docstring tweak. Tests pass (558 passed), manually confirmed ResourceWarning gone. patch.txt written.
